summary: Fixed two issues in the entry-signal engine. (1) Bar fetch (scripts/compute_indicators.py fetch_5min_bars + fetch_daily_bars) was passing Alpaca's `limit`, which returns the OLDEST bars in the window — so RSI/EMA/MACD/EMA-200 and watchlist `close` were computed off days-stale bars (NVDA was on last Friday's data). Now end-anchored: fetches the full window / tails the dailies, so indicators use current bars. (2) rel_vol redefined as time-of-day RVOL (lib/indicators.time_of_day_rvol): today's cumulative volume vs the average cumulative volume up to the same time of day over the prior RVOL_LOOKBACK_DAYS (14) sessions — feed-agnostic, fixing the structurally-low IEX readings that were the binding constraint rejecting ~23/25 names. Verified live: NVDA 1.08, AAPL 1.52, MBUU 1.72, MCY 0.12 (vs old broken 0.196 / 2.37). VWAP now resets per session. Added config INDICATOR_INTRADAY_LOOKBACK_DAYS=20, RVOL_LOOKBACK_DAYS=14. Legacy compute_relative_volume / apply_all_intraday signatures kept for back-compat; all 36 tests pass. No trade decisions made this run.

observations: First day of deployed capital and first completed trade: MPC entered midday on a clean RVOL-2.04 large-cap breakout but never extended — it drifted to ~break-even/slightly red and was closed at EOD (sold 18 @ 268.00, -29.70 / -0.61%) for failing two safe-hold gates (not profitable, trailing stop never activated). The miss was structural: MPC is a very low-ATR name (atr_pct ~0.05-0.08), so the partial-profit and trail triggers sat only pennies above entry and the stop only ~0.12% below, leaving no room for the trade to work intraday. Account net -9.79 (-0.01%) vs SPY +0.46% (alpha -0.47% on the day, but Bull is still fractionally ahead cumulatively). Takeaway for tomorrow: favor entries with more ATR headroom so partial/trail mechanics are reachable; book is flat overnight, regime calm (VIX ~16.5, SPY up).

Discovered an untracked -18 MPC SHORT in the live Alpaca account (not in positions.json). Root cause: the original MPC long's protective stop FILLED at 2026-06-04 16:38:02Z (18 @ 268.977778), closing the long ~1 min after the 16:37 entry, but that fill was never reconciled into positions.json. At EOD the review agent read the stale 'open long' state and sold 18 @ 268.00 to 'close' it -- with no long remaining, that opened an unintended -18 short, which then sat UNPROTECTED (no stop) overnight and into today. Action taken: bought-to-cover 18 @ 266.57 at ~10:20 ET today; account now FLAT with no open orders. Corrected P&L: true long result -12.10 (stop), accidental short +25.74 (MPC kept falling), net MPC episode +13.64 (vs the -29.70 the EOD journal had logged). Appended RECONCILE + two corrective EXIT records to trade_log.jsonl. UNDERLYING BUG to fix in code: place_order/EOD-close logic does not detect when a broker-side stop order self-fills, so positions.json drifts out of sync with the broker and a subsequent 'close' can flip flat->short. Recommend reconciling positions.json against tc.get_all_positions() before any close, and verifying side/qty before submitting sells.

observations: Book was FLAT all day and at EOD (positions.json {}), so zero overnight-hold decisions and nothing to close before the bell. The day's only 'completed trade' was the morning corrective BUY-TO-COVER of the unintended -18 MPC short left over from the 06-04 EOD double-sell (+$25.74 / +0.54%) -- state repair, not strategy edge, so the 1W tally is misleading noise. What actually drove the day: the tape opened calm but turned hard risk-off (SPY -2.49%), internals broke down by midday (widespread EMA9<EMA21, RSI 20s-30s), and the open + midday agents correctly forced no entries from a low-quality YTD-momentum watchlist. Watch for Monday: VIX likely up after the risk-off close; re-check regime premarket and keep the still-open state-reconciliation code bug in mind.

weekly_observations: Inception week (06-01..06-05): Bull finished ~flat (-0.01%) while SPY fell -2.77%, banking +2.76% relative alpha entirely by holding cash through Fridays hard risk-off (SPY -2.49% intraday) rather than from trading edge. The only deployed trade was MPC, and neither of the two logged exits is a clean signal-driven round-trip: the long stopped out ~1 min after entry (-12.10) because it was a very low-ATR name with a stop only ~0.12% below entry (no room for partial/trail mechanics), and the +25.74 winner is just the corrective cover of an unintended short the 06-04 EOD double-sell created — state repair, not strategy. So effective clean-signal sample is still ~0 and win-rate/indicator_stats are noise; do not draw strategy conclusions yet. What is genuinely working is the reject side of the gate: across ~6 open/midday checks on a low-quality YTD-momentum watchlist, zero forced entries, and selectivity beat SPY two straight sessions. For Monday 06-08: (1) re-check regime premarket — VIX likely ticked up after Fridays risk-off, do not assume the calm uptrend persists; if SPY is still below short-term EMAs treat it as down/choppy and demand intraday confirmation. (2) Demand ATR headroom on entries so partial/trail triggers are reachable (the MPC lesson). (3) The positions.json-vs-broker reconciliation code bug is STILL OPEN — reconcile against tc.get_all_positions() and verify side/qty before any close so a self-filled stop cannot flip flat->short again. Book is flat, account healthy (~$100,007, no PDT, kill switch clear).
